vocabulary_spotlight.gloss: To go — a core verb we'll move through the present, past and future.
vocabulary_spotlight.culture_note: Chale, Twi keeps it simple — you don't change the whole verb like in English. You just add a tiny marker: 're' for right now (-ing), 'bɛ' for the future (will), and you stretch the vowel for the past. Master 'kɔ' and you can swap in any verb.

grammar_mechanics.focus: Present, past & future tense with subject conjugation
grammar_mechanics.explanation: Akan verbs stay mostly the same — the subject pronoun and a small marker do the work. Subjects: me (I), wo (you), ɔ (he/she), yɛn (we), mo (you all), wɔn (they). The bare verb is the everyday/habitual present: 'Me kɔ' = I go. Add 're-' for the present continuous: 'Me rekɔ' = I am going. Lengthen the vowel for the past: kɔ → kɔɔ, so 'Me kɔɔ' = I went. Add 'bɛ-' for the future: 'Me bɛkɔ' = I will go.
patterns: Present: Me kɔ (I go) · Me rekɔ (I am going)
Subjects: me · wo · ɔ · yɛn · mo · wɔn

lineage_challenges.slang_hint: 'bɛ' points to the future — think 'bɛ' = 'be (about to)'.

lineage_challenges.prompt: Which sentence is in the PAST tense?
lineage_challenges.slang_hint: Past tense stretches the vowel: kɔ → kɔɔ.

lineage_challenges.prompt: Which marker makes the present continuous ('am …-ing')?
Which marker shows the FUTURE tense ('will')?

lineage_challenges.slang_hint: 'yɛn' is the 'we' subject — it shows up everywhere, so lock it in.

lineage_challenges.prompt: Using 'didi' (to eat), how do you say 'I will eat'?
lineage_challenges.slang_hint: Same recipe as 'kɔ': add 'bɛ' for the future — Me bɛdidi.
